_comment: Franchise Navigator data file — place at /franchises/bakugan.json
description: Hybrid marble/card-battle franchise by Sega Toys and Spin Master (2006). Spring-loaded spheres pop open into monster forms on magnetic gate cards — part marble game, part TCG, part creature collector. Three anime generations (2007–2023) and five retail games across DS, PSP, Wii, PS2, PS3, Xbox 360, and Switch.

media.title: Bakugan Battle Brawlers
media.description: Dan Kuso and the Battle Brawlers fight to save the dimension of Vestroia from the rogue Bakugan Naga and his ally Masquerade.
media.notes: Gen 1 start. Watch before playing the Battle Brawlers game. Amazon Prime Video.

media.title: Bakugan Battle Brawlers
media.description: Card-rolling battle game with an original story featuring exclusive Bakugan Leonidas, not present in the anime.
media.notes: DS recommended (melonds) — touchscreen gate cards feel natural. PS3 adds SIXAXIS tilt (RPCS3: BLES00758 / BLUS30281, 60 FPS patch available). Best first game.

media.title: Bakugan: New Vestroia
media.description: Dan returns to find Vestroia occupied by the Vexos, led by the enigmatic Spectra Phantom.
media.notes: Best Gen 1 season. Watch before Defenders of the Core — Spectra is the antagonist in both. Amazon Prime Video.

media.title: Bakugan: Battle Trainer
media.description: Simplified DS training spin-off with no story, designed to introduce card-rolling mechanics to younger players.
media.notes: melonds. ~45 min. No story. Optional — completionists only. Insert anywhere during Gen 1.

media.title: Bakugan: Defenders of the Core
media.description: 3D arena action-brawler set in the New Vestroia era; Spectra and the Vexos are the antagonists despite the misleading title.
media.notes: Best game in the franchise. PS3 via RPCS3 is best fidelity (Vulkan renderer, capture 20 Bakugan in Battle Mode). PSP via ppsspp runs in-browser — best mobile/browser option. Play after watching New Vestroia S2.

media.title: Bakugan: Dimensions
media.description: Browser-based online MMO where DNA codes from physical toys unlocked in-game Bakugan; US, Canada, and Australia only.
media.notes: DEFUNCT — shut down permanently June 30, 2011. No private server exists. Cannot be played. Historical record only.

media.title: Bakugan: Gundalian Invaders
media.description: The Brawlers are drawn into an interplanetary war between the alien worlds of Neathia and Gundalia.
media.notes: Weakest Gen 1 season but required for Mechtanium Surge context. Amazon Prime Video.

media.title: Bakugan: Rise of the Resistance
media.description: Tower defense game set in New Vestroia where players command Bakugan defensively across 30+ levels.
media.notes: DS only (melonds). ~5–7 hrs. Unique genre for the franchise. Play before Gundalian Invaders S3 for best story context.

media.title: Bakugan: Mechtanium Surge
media.description: The Gen 1 series finale in which Dan and Drago face the Mechtogan threat and Dan's arc reaches its conclusion.
media.notes: Gen 1 conclusion. Divisive among fans but closes Dan and Drago's full five-season arc. Amazon Prime Video.

media.title: BakuTech! Bakugan
media.description: Japan-only spin-off set in the BakuTech toy sub-line universe with an entirely different cast from the main series.
media.notes: Never dubbed into English. Skip unless you read Japanese. Not part of Gen 1 main continuity.

media.title: BakuTech! Bakugan Gachi
media.description: Sequel season to BakuTech! Bakugan, continuing the Japan-only spin-off with the same cast.

media.title: Bakugan: Battle Planet
media.studio: TMS Entertainment / Nelvana / Spin Master Entertainment
media.description: Full continuity reboot: new Dan Kouzo and friends are ViewTubers who stumble onto biomechanical Bakugan creatures.
media.notes: Gen 2 start. Lighter tone, shorter episode format. Peacock.

media.title: Bakugan: Armored Alliance
media.studio: TMS Entertainment / Nelvana / Spin Master Entertainment
media.description: Continuous Gen 2 sequel introducing the Baku-Gear armor system for Bakugan.
media.notes: Gen 2 S2. Watch straight through from Battle Planet. Peacock.

media.title: Bakugan: Champions of Vestroia
media.description: 3v3 action RPG with an original story set in the Gen 2 universe; players collect and evolve 80+ Bakugan.
media.notes: Switch only. Emulate via Yuzu (lower VRAM) or Ryujinx (more stable) on PC. ~3.2 GB NSP/XCI. Stream via Sunshine + Moonlight for mobile. Play after all five Gen 2 anime seasons.

media.title: Bakugan: Geogan Rising
media.studio: TMS Entertainment / Nelvana / Spin Master Entertainment
media.description: The Awesome Brawlers encounter Geogan — crystal-fused Bakugan with geometric forms and exceptional power.

media.title: Bakugan: Evolutions
media.studio: TMS Entertainment / Nelvana / Spin Master Entertainment
media.description: The connection between Earth and Vestroia begins to deteriorate, raising the stakes for the Awesome Brawlers.

media.title: Bakugan: Legends
media.studio: TMS Entertainment / Nelvana / Spin Master Entertainment
media.description: The Gen 2 series finale resolving the Earth–Vestroia storyline and the Awesome Brawlers' arc.
media.notes: Gen 2 conclusion. Netflix. Watch before playing Champions of Vestroia.

media.title: Bakugan (2023)
media.description: Gen 3 reboot following a new Dan across a six-planet Vestroian galaxy; cancelled after one season with no narrative resolution.
media.notes: CANCELLED — Spin Master killed the toy line mid-run. Unresolved ending, no second season ordered. Netflix / Disney XD.

media.title: Bakugan (Live-Action Film)
media.description: Announced live-action theatrical film adaptation of the Bakugan franchise.
media.notes: Announced Feb 6, 2025. Director / writer / producer: Brad Peyton (San Andreas, Rampage). No studio, cast, or confirmed release date as of last update. Year is placeholder.
